methodsAttempted.query: site:tu.berlin generative AI artificial intelligence policy students assessment TU Berlin
methodsAttempted.notes: Official TU Berlin search results surfaced the BZHL AI in university teaching page, examination-related pages, and official static PDFs.

methodsAttempted.query: site:tu.berlin KI Lehre Studium Prüfungen Richtlinie Technische Universität Berlin ChatGPT
methodsAttempted.notes: German-language discovery found the Referat Prüfungen AI-tools page, BZHL teaching guidance, Studyguide AI page, and department-only DIMA statement.

methodsAttempted.query: TU Berlin KI-Leitlinien linked from official examination and ChatGPT Edu pages
methodsAttempted.notes: The official TU pages repeatedly linked the ZEWK TU digit living AI guidelines page as TU Berlin guidance for teaching, exams, privacy, and scientific work.

robots.txt was checked. www.tu.berlin allowed the direct OpenClaw user agent under User-agent: * for the selected public paths; AI crawler agents including FirecrawlAgent are separately disallowed, so final source fetches used direct HTTP rather than Firecrawl snapshots.
robots.txt disallowed only /staging/ for User-agent: *; the public KI guidelines page was fetched directly.
robots.txt allowed the /datenschutz_notizen/ path used for the official data-protection blog evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found TU Berlin pages for student AI-use rules, ZEWK living AI guidelines, BZHL teaching guidance, student advising, ChatGPT Edu pilot access, and data-protection guidance. Department-only and hub/training pages were recorded as rejected for broad claim evidence where they could overstate university-wide policy scope.

stopConditions: Stop after citation-ready evidence is collected from official TU Berlin public pages.
Do not use department-only or generic hub pages for broad university-wide claims.
Do not access login-gated ISIS courses or protected TU pages.
Do not stage raw HTML, PDFs, screenshots, browser profiles, or logs.

artifacts.claimText: TU Berlin examination guidance tells students they remain fully responsible for all AI-generated content; errors or inaccuracies in AI output count as the student's errors, and AI may support work but may not take over the actual work.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: TU Berlin examination guidance states a general documentation duty when AI is used for assignments: students should identify the tools, extent, purpose, marked AI content, prompts, and their own contribution.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For final and seminar papers, TU Berlin examination guidance says AI-supported passages must be marked, except spelling checks and stylistic improvements, and that use of AI tools must be discussed with the supervisor.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: TU Berlin's living AI guidelines say personal data may be entered into generative AI tools only when the operator neither makes the data accessible to third parties nor uses it as training data; confidential information, sensitive research data, and internal documents are not permitted inputs.

citation.citationTitle: ki:richtlinien [TU digit | Das Wiki zu digitalen Kompetenzen und Medienproduktion an der TU Berlin]
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: TU Berlin's living AI guidelines say fully automated assessment of study or examination performance is not permissible; AI output may not be adopted one-to-one and digital tools such as AI may serve only as aids.

citation.citationTitle: ki:richtlinien [TU digit | Das Wiki zu digitalen Kompetenzen und Medienproduktion an der TU Berlin]
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: TU Berlin's living AI guidelines say that if AI results are adopted in scientific work, they must be checked for content, revised, and labeled; uses without relevance to learning or epistemic work, such as grammar, style, spelling, or translation, usually do not need to be declared.

citation.citationTitle: ki:richtlinien [TU digit | Das Wiki zu digitalen Kompetenzen und Medienproduktion an der TU Berlin]
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: TU Berlin's ChatGPT Edu pilot page describes scoped institutional access: the pilot began with Faculty VII, lists EU-server use, TUB-account/pseudo-ID login, and research/teaching/administration use, and says the licenses were extended with all faculties participating and 790 licenses total.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: TU Berlin's data-protection blog recommends limiting personal data in ChatGPT, not sharing confidential information, avoiding personal data especially about third parties, and disabling chat history because otherwise communication is stored long term and may feed training data depending on settings.

citation.citationTitle: chatGPT und der Datenschutz - Datenschutz-Blog Unter dem Radar

artifacts.sourceTitle: Verwendung von KI-Tools - TU Berlin
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Für alle durch KI generierten Inhalte tragen Sie die volle Verantwortung. Fehler oder Ungenauigkeiten in KI-Ergebnissen gelten als Ihre Fehler und nicht die des Tools. KI kann Sie unterstützen – etwa bei Ideenfindung, Recherche oder Strukturierung – darf aber nicht die eigentliche Arbeit übernehmen.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students are fully responsible for AI-generated content; AI can support idea generation, research, or structuring but must not take over the actual work.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section before Regeln für Übungen und Hausaufgaben
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Verwendung von KI-Tools - TU Berlin
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Generell gilt eine Pflicht zur Dokumentation, wenn Sie KI zur Bearbeitung von Aufgaben verwenden: Geben Sie stets an, welche Tools Sie genutzt haben, in welchem Umfang und zu welchem Zweck ... Falls Sie KI-Inhalte ... verwenden, kennzeichnen Sie diese und benennen Sie die verwendeten Prompts.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The page sets a documentation duty for AI use in assignments, including tools, extent, purpose, marked AI content, and prompts.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Verwendung von KI-Tools - TU Berlin
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Alle durch KI unterstützten Passagen müssen kenntlich gemacht sein (z. B. durch Fußnote) – Ausgenommen sind Rechtschreibprüfungen und stilistische Verbesserungen. ... In jedem Fall müssen Sie die Verwendung von KI-Tools mit Ihrer:m Betreuer:in absprechen!
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: AI-supported passages in final/seminar papers must be marked, with spelling and stylistic improvements excepted; AI-tool use must be discussed with the supervisor.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Regeln und Hinweise für Abschluss- und Seminararbeiten
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: ki:richtlinien [TU digit | Das Wiki zu digitalen Kompetenzen und Medienproduktion an der TU Berlin]
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Jedoch dürfen personenbezogene Daten nur in generative KI-Tools eingegeben werden, wenn die Softwarebetreiber diese Daten weder Dritten zugänglich machen noch als Trainingsdaten nutzen. Über den Datenschutz hinaus ist auch die Eingabe vertraulicher Informationen, sensibler Forschungsdaten oder interner Dokumente nicht zulässig.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Personal data can be entered only if the provider does not expose it to third parties or use it for training; confidential, sensitive research, or internal material is not permitted.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: ki:richtlinien [TU digit | Das Wiki zu digitalen Kompetenzen und Medienproduktion an der TU Berlin]
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Eine vollständig automatisierte Bewertung von Studien- oder Prüfungsleistungen ist nicht zulässig (basierend auf Art. 22 DSGVO). Das heißt, der Output darf nicht eins zu eins übernommen werden, sondern digitale Tools wie KI dürfen nur als Hilfsmittel dienen.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Fully automated grading of study or exam performance is not permissible; AI may serve only as an aid.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Konzeption & Didaktik / Eignet sich generative KI zur Bewertung von studentischen Leistungen?
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: ki:richtlinien [TU digit | Das Wiki zu digitalen Kompetenzen und Medienproduktion an der TU Berlin]
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Wenn jedoch KI-Ergebnisse übernommen werden sollen, müssen diese inhaltlich geprüft, überarbeitet und gekennzeichnet werden. ... KI-Nutzungen ohne Relevanz ... müssen meistens nicht angegeben werden („beispielsweise Grammatik-, Stil-, Rechtschreibprüfung, Übersetzungsprogramme“ ...).
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: AI results adopted in scientific work must be checked, revised, and labeled; grammar/style/spelling/translation uses usually need not be declared.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Pilotprojekt ChatGPT Edu - TU Berlin
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Datenschutzkonforme Nutzung auf Servern innerhalb der EU; Sichere Anmeldung mit TUB-Account und Pseudo-ID; Nutzung in Forschung, Lehre & Verwaltung erlaubt ... Die Lizenzen werden um ein weiteres Jahr verlängert ... alle anderen Fakultäten der TU Berlin beteiligt und die Gesamtzahl der Lizenzen auf 790 erhöht.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The page presents scoped ChatGPT Edu access with EU servers, TUB account/pseudo-ID login, use in research/teaching/admin, and 790 licenses after extension.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: chatGPT und der Datenschutz - Datenschutz-Blog Unter dem Radar
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Geben Sie nur wenige persönliche Daten preis ... Vertrauliche Informationen sollten nicht über chatGPT geteilt werden. ... sollten keine personenbezogenen Daten (insbesondere Dritter!) an chatGPT übermittelt werden. ... Chathistorie deaktiviert werden, denn andernfalls wird die Kommunikation langfristig gespeichert und fließt je nach gewählten Einstellungen als Trainingsdaten mit ein.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The data-protection blog recommends limiting personal/confidential ChatGPT inputs and disabling chat history to reduce long-term storage and training-data use.

artifacts.title: Technische Universität Berlin (TU Berlin) staged AI policy evidence - 2026-05-14
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source evidence for TU Berlin covers student responsibility for AI-generated content, documentation and marking of AI use, privacy limits for personal/confidential data, prohibition of fully automated assessment, checked/labeled AI output in scientific work, scoped ChatGPT Edu pilot access, and data-protection blog recommendations. Department-only and hub/training pages were rejected for broad claim evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The ChatGPT Edu claim is scoped to the official pilot page and should not be read as unrestricted university-wide access for every TU Berlin user.
The DIMA chair statement was not used for broad TU Berlin policy claims because it is department/chair-specific.
No login-gated ISIS course or protected TU Berlin page was accessed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
